titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Bodkin and Arnold F. Graves to the Under Secretary, 1 August, 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Bodkin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Thomas Bodkin and Arnold F. Graves, Secretaries of the Commissioners of Charitable Donations and Bequests for Ireland to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), the under secretary for Ireland. Chalmers succeeded Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), who resigned on 3 May, 1916 due to the administration's not preventing the Easter Rising. This is a cover letter which accompanied the 71st report for the Commissioners of Charitable Donations and Bequests for Ireland.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13029

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Commissioners of Charitable Donations and Bequests for Ireland. 2 KILDARE PLACE, DUBLIN, 1st day of August 1916 Sir, By direction of the Board, we have the honour to
transmit herewith five copies of its 71st Report, one
being for presenting to their Excellencies, the Lords
Justices. In accordance with the usual custom three copies
have been forwarded to the Home Secretary for presenting
to His Majesty the King, and two to be laid before both
Houses of Parliament pursuant to the 7th and 8th Victoria
Cap 97, Sec 10. We have the honour to be
Sir
Your Obedient Servants Thomas Bodkin Arnold F. Graves
Secretaries. The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle.
